LoanPage.metaDescription: Calculate your monthly payment and total interest for equal payment, equal principal, and bullet (interest-only) repayment plans.
LoanPage.subtitle: Calculate interest for equal payment, equal principal, and bullet repayment plans
LoanPage.guideEqualInstallmentDesc: Pay the same amount every month. Predictable monthly payment.
LoanPage.guideEqualPrincipalDesc: Pay the same principal every month. Relatively lower total interest.
LoanPage.guideBalloonDesc: Pay only interest during the term, then repay the full principal at maturity.
LoanPage.formulaEqualInstallmentBody: Monthly payment = principal × monthly rate × (1+monthly rate)^months ÷ {(1+monthly rate)^months − 1}. Monthly rate is the annual rate divided by 12. You pay the same amount every month, but the interest portion is larger early on and the principal portion grows over time.
LoanPage.formulaEqualPrincipalBody: The principal paid each month = loan principal ÷ number of months, and stays constant. Interest is calculated as the remaining balance × monthly rate, so as the balance shrinks, the monthly payment decreases over time.
LoanPage.formulaBalloonBody: You pay only interest (principal × monthly rate) each month, then repay the full principal plus the final month's interest in one lump sum at maturity. Since the principal never decreases, total interest is the highest of the three plans.
LoanPage.exampleTitle: Example (30,000,000 KRW principal · 5% annual rate · 36 months)
LoanPage.exampleEqualInstallment: Equal Payment: about 899,127 KRW × 36 payments → total interest 2,368,570 KRW, total payment 32,368,572 KRW
LoanPage.exampleEqualPrincipal: Equal Principal: starts at 958,333 KRW and decreases each month → total interest 2,312,500 KRW, total payment 32,312,500 KRW
LoanPage.exampleBalloon: Bullet: 125,000 KRW interest-only each month, plus the full 30,000,000 KRW principal at the final month → total interest 4,500,000 KRW, total payment 34,500,000 KRW
LoanPage.exampleNote: Under identical terms, total interest can differ by up to about 2.13M KRW depending on the repayment plan. Equal principal has the lowest total interest but the highest initial burden; bullet repayment has the lowest initial burden but the highest total interest.

faq.q: Which is better, equal payment or equal principal?
faq.a: Equal principal results in lower total interest, but its early monthly payments are much higher than equal payment. If your income is steady, equal payment is easier to manage; if you can afford higher payments early on, equal principal saves more overall.

faq.q: How much interest do I save by repaying early?
faq.a: Excluding any early repayment fee, you save interest on the remaining principal going forward. Repaying early in the loan term saves more. This calculator shows the standard schedule and does not account for early repayment.

faq.q: Should I choose a variable or fixed interest rate?
faq.a: Variable rates move with the market, while fixed rates stay the same for the full term. A fixed rate can be advantageous if rates are expected to rise, and a variable rate if they're expected to fall.

faq.q: What happens to total interest if I extend the loan term?
faq.a: A longer term lowers your monthly payment but increases total interest. In the example above, extending from 36 to 60 months lowers the monthly payment but raises the total interest paid.

faq.q: Why do the results differ from my actual bank loan?
faq.a: Banks vary in origination fees, early repayment penalties, and preferential rate discounts, which can cause real repayment amounts to differ. Check with your lender for exact terms.

BmiPage.metaDescription: Enter your height and weight to calculate your Body Mass Index (BMI) and weight category.
BmiPage.subtitle: Check your Body Mass Index and weight category from height and weight
BmiPage.formulaBody: BMI = weight (kg) ÷ [height (m)]². For example, at 170cm (1.7m) and 65kg: BMI = 65 ÷ (1.7 × 1.7) = 65 ÷ 2.89 ≈ 22.5, which falls in the normal range.
BmiPage.categoryNote: The Asia-Pacific standard classifies lower BMI values as at-risk compared to the general WHO standard. This calculator uses the Asia-Pacific standard, similar to the Korean Society for the Study of Obesity's criteria.
BmiPage.limitationsBody: Because BMI is calculated from height and weight alone, it cannot distinguish muscle mass from body fat. A muscular athlete may show a high BMI despite low body fat, while a person with so-called "skinny fat" body composition may have a normal BMI but a high body fat percentage. For a more accurate assessment, consider pairing BMI with a body fat measurement (e.g., a bioelectrical impedance scan).

faq.q: My BMI is normal but my body fat percentage is high — why?
faq.a: This is often called "skinny fat" — low muscle mass with a relatively high body fat ratio. BMI alone can't reveal body composition, so a body fat measurement is needed alongside it.

faq.q: Do adults and children/teens use the same BMI standard?
faq.a: No. People under 18 use age- and sex-specific percentile charts instead. This calculator uses the adult standard.

faq.q: Why does having more muscle raise my BMI?
faq.a: BMI only accounts for total body weight, so muscle weighs the same as fat in the calculation. That's why a muscular person with low body fat can still show as overweight or obese by BMI.

faq.q: Do Asian and Western populations use different BMI thresholds?
faq.a: Yes. Unlike the general WHO standard, the Asia-Pacific standard treats lower BMI values as risk thresholds at the same body fat percentage. This calculator uses the Asia-Pacific standard.

faq.q: Can pregnant women use this calculator?
faq.a: During pregnancy, weight gain is a normal part of the process, so standard BMI categories don't apply well. Consult an OB-GYN for weight management guidance during pregnancy.

BmiCalculator.underAdvice: Gaining some weight may help improve your health.
BmiCalculator.normalAdvice: Maintain your current weight. You're in a healthy range.
BmiCalculator.overAdvice: Diet control and regular exercise are recommended.
BmiCalculator.obese1Advice: Consult a doctor before starting a weight loss plan.
BmiCalculator.obese2Advice: Getting help from a medical professional is recommended.

UnitPage.metaDescription: Convert length, weight, temperature, area, and other units instantly.
UnitPage.subtitle: Easily convert length, weight, temperature, and area units
UnitPage.formulaTemperatureBody: Celsius to Fahrenheit: °F = °C × 9/5 + 32. Fahrenheit to Celsius: °C = (°F − 32) × 5/9. Celsius to Kelvin: K = °C + 273.15

faq.q: How many square meters is one pyeong exactly?
faq.a: 1 pyeong = 3.30579m². It's a traditional Korean unit commonly used in real estate listings — not an SI unit, but still widely used in Korea.

faq.q: Why is the Celsius-to-Fahrenheit formula so complex?
faq.a: Celsius is based on water's freezing point (0°C) and boiling point (100°C), while Fahrenheit uses a different reference, so both a scaling factor (9/5) and an offset (32) are needed to convert between them.

faq.q: Why do mile-to-kilometer conversions vary slightly between calculators?
faq.a: The internationally standardized value for 1 mile is 1,609.344m; different calculators round decimals differently, producing tiny discrepancies.

faq.q: Is an ounce (oz) a weight or volume unit?
faq.a: Ounce is used for both weight (mass) and volume (fluid) measurements. This calculator uses the weight unit (1oz ≈ 28.35g).

faq.q: When is an acre used?
faq.a: Acres are mainly used to describe land area in the US and UK. In Korea, it's occasionally used as a reference when describing golf courses or large plots of land.

SalaryPage.metaDescription: Enter your annual salary in Korea to instantly calculate monthly take-home pay after 4 major social insurances and income tax. Based on 2024 deduction rates.
SalaryPage.subtitle: Monthly take-home pay after social insurance and income tax deductions (based on 2024 rates, Korea)
SalaryPage.step2: National Pension = monthly salary × 4.5% (capped at 5.9M KRW monthly income)
SalaryPage.step4: Long-Term Care Insurance = health insurance premium × 12.95%
SalaryPage.step6: Income tax is calculated by applying the progressive tax rate (6–45%) to taxable income (after the earned income deduction and personal deductions), then subtracting the earned income tax credit
SalaryPage.step8: Take-home pay = monthly salary − (National Pension + Health Insurance + Long-Term Care + Employment Insurance + income tax + local tax)
SalaryPage.example1: Annual salary 40,000,000 KRW, 0 dependents: from a monthly salary of 3,333,333 KRW, deduct National Pension 149,999, Health Insurance 118,166, Long-Term Care 15,302, Employment Insurance 29,999, income tax 133,605, and local tax 13,360 — leaving a monthly take-home pay of about 2,872,902 KRW (annual take-home about 34,474,824 KRW).
SalaryPage.example2: Annual salary 60,000,000 KRW, 1 dependent: from a monthly salary of 5,000,000 KRW, total deductions of 825,056 KRW leave a monthly take-home pay of about 4,174,944 KRW (annual take-home about 50,099,328 KRW).
SalaryPage.rateNote: This calculator applies current deduction rates: National Pension 4.5%, Health Insurance 3.545%, Long-Term Care Insurance 12.95%, and Employment Insurance 0.9%. Rates change annually, so check official sources like the National Pension Service and National Tax Service for the most accurate figures.

faq.q: How does the number of dependents affect the result?
faq.a: Each dependent adds a 1.5M KRW personal deduction, which lowers taxable income and therefore income tax — increasing take-home pay.

faq.q: Does National Pension keep increasing with higher income?
faq.a: No. National Pension is capped at a monthly income of 5.9M KRW; income above that isn't subject to additional National Pension contributions.

faq.q: Why does take-home pay differ between companies at the same salary?
faq.a: While insurance and tax rates are the same everywhere, companies structure non-taxable allowances (meal allowance, vehicle allowance, etc.) differently, which changes taxable income and therefore take-home pay.

faq.q: Should I negotiate salary based on take-home pay?
faq.a: Salary is usually negotiated on a pre-tax basis, but since take-home pay is what you actually feel in your pocket, it's worth calculating it in advance before negotiating.

faq.q: Does this apply to freelancers or self-employed income?
faq.a: No. This calculator is based on employment income for workers enrolled in the 4 major social insurances. Freelancers are typically taxed differently, e.g., a flat 3.3% withholding on business income.

SalaryCalculator.localTax: Local Income Tax (10% of income tax)
SalaryCalculator.seoDesc1: Take-home pay is your pre-tax annual salary minus Korea's 4 major social insurances (national pension, health insurance, long-term care insurance, employment insurance) and income tax plus local income tax.
SalaryCalculator.seoDesc2: This calculator applies 2024 deduction rates. Actual take-home pay may vary depending on company policy and individual circumstances.

About.metaDescription: Calc Hub is a free online calculator and converter site anyone can use.
About.subtitle: Learn what Calc Hub is all about.
About.introBody: Calc Hub is a free collection of online tools that help you quickly and conveniently perform everyday calculations and conversions. No app installation needed — use it directly in your browser.
About.policy1: All services are provided free of charge.
About.policy2: Your input values are not stored on our servers.
About.policy3: Results are for reference only; consult a professional for important decisions.
About.policy4: We continuously add new tools to expand our services.
About.contactPrompt: If you have any questions or suggestions about our service, feel free to reach out.

Contact.metaDescription: Send us your questions about Calc Hub.
Contact.subtitle: Let us know about any issues, bugs, or suggestions.
Contact.name: Name
Contact.note: We'll reply by email within 1-3 business days.

Privacy.s1Body: Calc Hub ("the Site") values your privacy and complies with applicable data protection laws, including South Korea's Personal Information Protection Act. This policy explains what information the Site collects and how it is used.
Privacy.s2Intro: The Site does not directly collect personal information from users. However, the following information may be collected automatically.
Privacy.s2Item1: Usage statistics such as pages visited and time spent (Google Analytics)
Privacy.s2Item2: Cookies and interest-based data for serving ads (Google AdSense)
Privacy.s2Item3: Technical information such as IP address, browser type, and operating system
Privacy.s3Body1: The Site uses cookies through the Google AdSense advertising service. Cookies are small text files stored in your browser and used to personalize ads.
Privacy.s3Body2: You can refuse cookies in your browser settings. Note that disabling cookies may limit some site features.
Privacy.s4Body1: The Site displays ads through Google AdSense. Google uses cookies to serve ads based on your prior visits.
Privacy.s4Body2Pre: For more information about Google's advertising and privacy policies, see
Privacy.s5Body: The Site may contain links to external sites. The Site is not responsible for the privacy practices of external sites; please review their privacy policies separately.
Privacy.s6BodyPre: For questions about how we handle personal information, please contact us via the
Privacy.s7Body: This privacy policy may be updated to reflect changes in law or our services. Updates will be announced on this page.
